title: Finite Universal Coefficient Shadow Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite universal-coefficient shadow QF_UF/Alethe regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Finite replay computes H^1 = Z/2 for the dual cochain complex of d1=[2], while the malformed row asserts H^1 = 0 against a declared Z/2 != 0 distinction.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional universal-coefficient rows only when they add distinct Ext/Tor, exact-sequence, quotient, or proof-reconstruction pressure; keep the theorem schema itself in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: State the finite chain complex, dual cochain complex, homology invariants, cohomology invariants, Hom term, Ext term, and degree-one exact-sequence shadow explicitly.
Replay the integer cochain complex and group-invariant bookkeeping with exact integer arithmetic.
Keep the promoted bad H^1=0 row tied to a committed SMT-LIB artifact and checked QF_UF/Alethe evidence.
Add broader Ext/Tor or exact-sequence proof resources only after they have deterministic replay data and route regressions.
Keep the universal coefficient theorem, naturality, splitting choices, and arbitrary chain-complex statements under Lean horizon until kernel-checked proof routes exist.
